title: Part 2: Rate Limiting — the token bucket and the burst trade-off

cues.label: Full bucket
cues.headline: Capacity 5, refill 1/sec
cues.narration: The bucket holds up to a capacity of tokens and refills at a steady rate; each request spends one.

cues.label: Spend tokens
cues.headline: Three requests, three tokens
cues.narration: Three requests at once spend three tokens; all are allowed while the bucket has tokens.

cues.label: Burst empties it
cues.headline: Extra requests rejected
cues.narration: A burst drains the bucket to zero; once empty, further requests are rejected until it refills.

cues.label: Lazy refill
cues.headline: Elapsed × rate, capped
cues.narration: On the next request, elapsed time times the rate refills the tokens, capped at capacity — no timer.

cues.label: Sliding window
cues.headline: Strict rolling count
cues.narration: The sliding-window counter forbids bursts: it counts recent timestamps and allows only under the limit.

cues.label: Pick the tool
cues.headline: Burst vs smoothness
cues.narration: Token bucket for friendly bursts and an average; sliding window when bursts are forbidden.

formulas: \text{token bucket } O(1) \text{ time, } O(1) \text{ state per client}
